pattern: BFS/DFS from city 0 on an undirected view of the tree — an edge pointing away from 0 (in the original direction) costs 1 reversal; an edge pointing toward 0 costs 0.
hints: Build an undirected adjacency list but tag each edge with a direction cost: 1 if it points away from 0 in the original graph, 0 if it already points toward 0.
BFS from node 0 and accumulate the cost of each tree edge you traverse.
Because the graph is a tree (n nodes, n-1 edges), there is exactly one path from every node to 0, so BFS visits every edge exactly once.

edge_inputs.description: Two cities, road points away from 0 — one reversal
Two cities, road points toward 0 — zero reversals
All edges already point toward 0 — answer is 0
Statement example 3 — all edges already correct
Linear chain pointing away from 0 — need n-1 reversals
Linear chain pointing toward 0 — need 0 reversals

stress.description: Random tree of 50000 nodes with random edge directions
